title: Persist settings locally, auto-reconnect printer, and duplicate serial error

requirements.text: Persist all label settings (including barcode positions, dimensions, font sizes, and all other fields in labelSettingsStore) to localStorage and automatically rehydrate them when the app is opened. Ensure the Zustand store in labelSettingsStore.ts uses the `persist` middleware so state survives page reloads and app restarts.

quotes: Keep settings saved locally on device and load when app is opened

acceptanceCriteria: Label settings changed by the user are saved to localStorage immediately.
On app open/reload, labelSettingsStore rehydrates from localStorage and the UI reflects the saved values.
If no saved settings exist, defaults are applied as before.

requirements.text: Persist all prefix-to-label-type mappings (prefix settings) to localStorage and automatically load them when the app is opened. These are part of labelSettingsStore; confirm they are included in the persisted state and not excluded from the persistence layer.

quotes: Keep settings with prefix saved locally on device and load them automatically when app is opened

acceptanceCriteria: Prefix mappings configured by the user survive a page reload.
On app open, the prefix mappings are restored from localStorage and rendered correctly in the Label Settings tab.
New prefix mappings added by the user are persisted immediately.

requirements.text: When a USB printer is successfully connected, save the printer's USB vendor ID and product ID (and any other identifying information available from WebUSB) to localStorage. On app open, automatically attempt to reconnect to the last connected printer using the stored identifiers. Show the connection result (success or failure) using the existing printer status badge in the header.

quotes: Connect automatically to last connected printer

acceptanceCriteria: After a successful USB printer connection, the printer's identifying details are saved to localStorage.
On app open, if saved printer details exist, a reconnection attempt is made automatically without user interaction.
If reconnection succeeds, the printer status badge shows connected.
If reconnection fails (device not available), a non-blocking error is logged and the user can manually connect from the Devices tab.
If no prior printer was ever connected, no auto-connect attempt is made.

requirements.text: In the ScanPrintTab two-step scanning workflow, after the 2nd serial number is scanned, check if it is exactly equal to the 1st serial number (after normalization via serialNormalization.ts). If they are identical, display a prominent inline error message instructing the user to scan a unique serial number, and play the error sound using the existing soundSystem.ts error sound. Do not proceed with printing. Clear the 2nd serial field so the user can re-scan.

quotes: When scanning 2nd serial number if is exactly the same as 1st show error message and make sound notification to san unique serial number

acceptanceCriteria: When the 2nd scanned serial is exactly the same as the 1st (after normalization), an error message is shown inline in the scan workflow (e.g., 'Serial numbers must be unique — please scan a different serial number.').
The error sound from soundSystem.ts is triggered immediately when the duplicate is detected.
The print action is blocked; no CPCL is generated or sent to the printer.
The 2nd serial input is cleared so the user can scan again.
The 1st serial remains set so the user does not need to re-scan it.
When the 2nd serial is different from the 1st, the workflow proceeds normally.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All persistence must use localStorage only — no backend changes required.
Auto-reconnect to printer must use WebUSB APIs already used in usbCpclTransport.ts and printerService.ts.
Sound notification for duplicate serial must use the existing soundSystem.ts error sound mechanism.
Serial comparison for duplicate detection must use serialNormalization.ts before comparing.
nonGoals: No new backend endpoints or Motoko changes.
No cloud sync or cross-device settings sharing.
No changes to the authentication or lock screen flow.
No new sound files — use the existing error sound event from soundSystem.ts.
